financials.role: financial statement cross-check candidate; not valuation SSOT

overview.description: Alphabet Inc. offers various products and platforms in the United States, Europe, the Middle East, Africa, the Asia-Pacific, Canada, and Latin America. It operates through Google Services, Google Cloud, and Other Bets segments. The Google Services segment provides products and services, including ads, Android, Chrome, devices, Gmail, Google Drive, Google Maps, Google Photos, Google Play, Search, and YouTube. It is also involved in the sale of apps and in-app purchases and digital content in Google Play and YouTube; and devices, as well as the p...
overview.financialIntro: In 2025, Alphabet's revenue was $402.84 billion, an increase of 15.09% compared to the previous year's $350.02 billion. Earnings were $132.17 billion, an increase of 32.01%.
overview.analystIntro: According to 64 analysts, the average rating for GOOGL stock is "Strong Buy." The 12-month stock price target is $432.29, which is an increase of 19.69% from the latest price.

data.title: Waymo starts driverless rides in four more U.S. markets as expansion accelerates
data.text: Waymo said it will begin offering robotaxi rides in four new U.S. cities: San Diego, Las Vegas, Tampa, Florida, and Denver. The company will first start offering rides to employees in the coming weeks...

data.title: Netflix, Disney, and YouTube Could Challenge Fox for World Cup Rights
data.text: Entertainment firms Netflix ($NFLX), Disney ($DIS), and YouTube ($GOOGL) are reportedly interested in challenging Fox ($FOXA) for the U.S. broadcast rights to the 2030 and 2034 men’s FIFA World Cup.

data.title: Google's Pixel event is set for August 12
data.text: Google is preparing for its Made by Google launch event, which is scheduled for August 12 in New York City, as announced on Tuesday.

data.text: Magnetic fusion start-up Proxima Fusion has raised €411 million ($469.69 million) from investors including Alphabet's ​Google and German utility RWE as ‌part of a financing round, the Munich-based sta...

data.title: Google backs nuclear fusion startup targeting Europe's first commercial power plant
data.text: Google backed Proxima Fusion in a €411 million, or $468 million, funding round. Proxima is targeting Europe's first commercial fusion power plant.

data.title: Google Expands AI Training to Include User-Uploaded Media via Search Tools
data.text: Google has expanded the scope of data it collects to train its artificial intelligence models, now incorporating media uploaded by users across several of its primary search-related services.

data.title: Why Wall Street Is Fired Up About Earnings—and Why There May Be Some Concern
data.text: Higher prices for raw materials and semiconductors were likely a boon to S&P 500 earnings last quarter. They could also be a risk.

data.title: Google's status as the dream tech job is changing. FOMO over AI salaries is only one reason why.
data.text: Google has long been regarded as a dream employer in tech. Some current and former employees say that reputation has become more complicated.

data.title: Waymo says some of its robotaxis ran out of power during San Francisco's July 4 gridlock and had to be towed
data.text: Some of Waymo's cars struggled to navigate the chaos of July 4 in San Francisco. Posts on X showed the company's vehicles stalled in traffic, adding to the gridlock.

data.title: Waymo robotaxis have a few easter eggs to look out for during your Fourth of July ride
data.text: Waymo makes slight adjustments to the in-car experience to celebrate holidays. For the Fourth of July, the company is deploying themed wrapped cars and a custom in-car display.

data.title: A warning sign about AI's real cost, courtesy of Google and Amazon
data.text: It's no secret that AI is a hog, consuming energy and water like no digital technology before it. Now we know just how much Big Tech's pursuit of AI is costing the environment.

data.title: Google disrupts NetNut proxy network used in malware operations
data.text: Alphabet's Google said on Thursday it weakened a large network of internet-connected devices that was ​being used to hide and route malicious ‌online activity.

data.title: Google loses fight against EU's record $4.7B fine over alleged antitrust practices
data.text: Google on Thursday lost its last bid to overturn a record-breaking $4.7 billion antitrust fine from the European Union, the latest blow as overseas regulators crack down on Big Tech.

data.title: EU top court upholds record 4.1 bn euro Google fine
data.text: The EU's top court on Thursday upheld a record 4.1 billion euro ($4.7 billion) fine the bloc slapped on Google for anticompetitive practices related to its Android operating system.

data.title: Thursday's Morning Movers: ADBE Upgrade, GOOGL PT Cut & Airline PT Hikes
data.text: At the opening bell, George Tsilis walks us through Thursday morning's top moving stocks. He says HSBC upgraded Adobe (ADBE) to buy and raised its price target on the beaten down software stock.

data.title: Google must pay record €4.1bn fine over antitrust issues
data.text: Google will have to pay a record €4.1bn (£3.5bn) fine after it lost its fight against EU antitrust regulators.

data.title: GOOGL, AMZN & META Price Target Adjustments, PLTR Upgrade Move Tech
data.text: Futures are muted ahead of the finally trading day of a holiday-shortened week. Tom White recaps the week explains why investors are likely taking a breather on moves ahead of the opening bell.
